nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=155 beats='Wren Callahan cannot sleep, so she climbs to the attic and switches on her late grandfather old shortwave radio; out of the hiss comes the same loop she heard last night, a womans voice reading a string of numbers, then a slow descending tone like a countdown that resets and begins again; three threads tug at her, the coordinates buried in the numbers, the dead relay station out on the headland, and the previous listener logbook her grandfather kept'>>

choices.label: Plot the coordinates the voice keeps reading.
Bike out to the abandoned relay station.
Dig out the previous listener logbook.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='Wren copies the numbers onto graph paper and treats them as latitude and longitude; two of them resolve to places she knows, the working harbor down the hill and the derelict water tower on the headland ridge, and she has to pick which to walk to before the batteries in her handheld die'>>

choices.label: Head down to the harbor.
Climb to the ridge water tower.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='The harbor is asleep, ropes ticking against masts; the coordinates point between the harbormaster hut, where a light still burns, and the old tide-gauge station on the breakwater that everyone says was shut years ago'>>

choices.label: Knock at the harbormaster hut.
Cross to the tide-gauge shed.
Check the pier notice-board.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='The pier notice-board is layered with faded flyers, but one index card is newer than the rest, a single frequency inked by a careful hand with the words if you hear the loop, it is real; someone has been leaving breadcrumbs for anyone who listens hard enough'>>

choices.label: Examine the card more closely.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='The card frequency matches the loop exactly, and the ink is barely dry; Wren can follow the slip as the deliberate clue it plainly is, or she can decide a weathered index card proves nothing and let the wind have it'>>

choices.label: Follow the pinned frequency slip.
Dismiss it as old junk and move on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='Wren tells herself a scrap of card means nothing and lets it flap loose into the dark; only later, lying awake, does she wonder whose careful hand wrote it, and by then the pier is a mile behind her and the loop is just a sound she used to chase'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The harbormaster, Mr. Pell, listens to the loop on Wren’s phone and frowns; he says a signal like that used to run off the headland relay, and that whoever kept it going was stubborn as barnacles, then he points her toward the truth without quite meaning to'>>

choices.label: Follow the thread back to the relay.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The tide-gauge shed is padlocked but the window is cracked; inside, a dusty receiver blinks in time with the loop, proof the signal reaches here too, and Wren weighs forcing the warped door against simply logging the frequency and going home'>>

choices.label: Force the warped door.
Just log the frequency and leave.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The door gives with a groan; the receiver is wired to a hand-drawn card that reads relay KX, and Wren realizes the harbor set is only an echo of something broadcasting from the headland'>>

choices.label: Trace it to the headland source.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Wren writes the frequency neatly in her notebook and walks home in the dark, telling herself she will come back with a plan; she never quite does, and the loop keeps counting down to no one, the harbor light blinking over an empty breakwater'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='The water tower stands rusted against the stars, a stub of antenna still bolted to its cap; the loop is stronger here, and Wren can either climb the maintenance ladder toward that antenna or search the padlocked service box at the base'>>

choices.label: Climb toward the antenna.
Search the service box at the base.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='Halfway up the cold ladder the wind shoves at Wren, but from the cap she sees a thin cable running off the tower and northeast along the ridge, straight toward the dark shape of the old relay station; the signal is not here, it only passes through'>>

choices.label: Follow the cable to the relay.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The service box is furred with rust; one panel is loose enough to pry, though it might take her whole night, while a faded arrow stenciled on the concrete simply points along the ridge toward the relay'>>

choices.label: Pry the rusted panel open.
Follow the stenciled arrow instead.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Inside the box is a junction and a laminated tag, KX RELAY, HEADLAND, do not disconnect; the tower is only a repeater, and the real transmitter is further out where the land ends'>>

choices.label: Head for the headland relay.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='The stenciled arrows lead Wren along a cracked service path toward the headland; whoever laid this trail wanted it followed, and the loop grows steadier with every step'>>

choices.label: Keep following the path.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Wren pieces the harbor and tower clues together on her graph paper; every arrow, every echo, points to one source out on the headland, and the countdown in her ear feels suddenly less like noise and more like a message with an address'>>

choices.label: Close in on the source.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='The last leg of the coordinate trail brings Wren to a wind-scoured overlook above the relay compound; the loop is loudest here, unmistakably alive, and whatever it is, it is close enough now to touch'>>

choices.label: Confront what is transmitting.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='Wren wheels her bike onto the headland road; the relay station squats at the cliff edge, its mast a black needle against the moon, and two ways lead in, the exposed coast path along the fence line or the flooded service lane that curves behind the compound'>>

choices.label: Take the coast path along the fence.
Take the service lane behind.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The coast path runs a hands-width from the fence and a long drop to the surf; the wind is vicious, and Wren spots a gap where the chain-link has peeled back, though part of her says to photograph the mast and retreat before she is blown off the ledge'>>

choices.label: Slip through the peeled-back gap.
Photograph the mast and turn back.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Wren squeezes through the gap into the compound; a weather-blistered sign reads RELAY KX, and a single window glows faintly, the same rhythm as the loop, someone or something inside still keeping it alive'>>

choices.label: Move toward the glowing window.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The gust nearly takes Wren off her feet, so she snaps three blurred photos of the mast and pedals home, heart hammering; the pictures show only a dark tower, and the loop plays on without her, a mystery she can describe but never solved'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The service lane is half under seawater but sheltered from the wind; ahead sits a gatehouse with a lit log board visible through the glass, while a side track detours to a squat transformer hut humming louder than the tide'>>

choices.label: Try the gatehouse and its log board.
Wade to the humming transformer hut.
Duck into the flooded cellar entrance.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='The cellar steps drop into ankle-deep seawater and the reek of brine; taped above the tide line is a plastic sleeve holding a single operator note, and the water is rising with every set of waves that booms against the sea wall outside'>>

choices.label: Reach for the taped note.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='The note reads, loop stays up while I draw breath, signed with the call sign KX; Wren can peel the sleeve free and pocket the proof, or she can feel the cold water climbing past her knees and scramble back before the cellar fills'>>

choices.label: Salvage the taped operator note.
Retreat from the rising water.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='The water wins the argument; Wren backs up the streaming steps as the cellar swallows the note whole, and she stands dripping in the yard with nothing to show but a glimpse of a call sign and the certainty that she was one wave too slow'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The gatehouse is unlocked and warm, as if just left; a wall board logs transmissions by hand in fresh ink, the last line only hours old, when a dog begins to bark somewhere in the dark and Wren must decide to read on or grab what she can and slip out'>>

choices.label: Study the handwritten log board.
Grab a torn log page and slip away.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The log is decades deep in one patient hand; the final entries repeat a single note, loop holding, still no answer, and Wren understands the signal is not automatic at all but tended by a real, waiting person'>>

choices.label: Carry the log toward the source.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='Wren pockets a torn page and ducks out as the barking closes in; under a streetlight she reads a single line, for M, still holding, and the loop stops being a machine and becomes a message somebody meant for someone'>>

choices.label: Chase down who M might be.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The transformer hut throbs with power feeding the mast; a maintenance sticker names the last technician who signed it, K. Alder, in handwriting Wren has already seen tonight on a dozen faded cards'>>

choices.label: Follow the name Alder inward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Standing in the dripping compound, Wren gathers what the station has told her; the loop is deliberate, tended, addressed, and it is counting toward something a real person set in motion and is still, somewhere, waiting on'>>

choices.label: Face the source of the signal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='Wren wipes the rain from her eyes and steps toward the one lit window; the loop pulses through the wall in time with the glow, and she knows that beyond this glass is the person who has been broadcasting into the dark all along'>>

choices.label: Confront what is transmitting.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=140 beats='Back in the house, Wren hunts for her grandfather logbook; he listened to the airwaves for forty years and wrote it all down, and the book could be in the cedar trunk in the attic or out in his radio shed with its wall of pinned notes'>>

choices.label: Open the cedar trunk in the attic.
Search the radio shed.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The trunk breathes out cedar and old paper; inside lie a water-stained logbook and a fat bundle of QSL cards, the postcards operators mailed to confirm a contact, and Wren can only read one carefully before her eyes give out'>>

choices.label: Read the water-stained logbook.
Sort through the QSL cards.
Untie the ribboned bundle of letters.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='Under the cards lies a ribboned bundle of letters her grandfather never sent, all addressed to the relay; the top one begins, Kell, she may still be out there, keep the channel open, and Wren realizes the two old men shared this vigil across the water for years'>>

choices.label: Read the letter more carefully.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=125 beats='The letter names Alder plainly and pleads with him not to give up on his daughter; it feels like reading a stranger heart, and Wren can press on because the loop needs an answer, or she can retie the ribbon, deciding some grief is not hers to open'>>

choices.label: Read the letter that names Alder.
Feel it is too private and stop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='Wren reknots the ribbon and sets the letters gently back, unwilling to pry into two old men sorrow; it is a kind choice and a final one, for the names and frequencies she needed were folded inside, and without them the trail simply ends in a drawer full of respect'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Her grandfather logged the KX loop for years, always with the same underlined note, ALDER still transmitting, why; the last page, dated the week he died, simply asks whether anyone will finally answer the poor man'>>

choices.label: Take up the question he left.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The QSL cards are from listeners all over the coast, all logging the same stubborn signal; one card, heavier than the rest, names an exact frequency and a call sign, KX ALDER, while the others are only friendly postcards she could set aside'>>

choices.label: Chase the card naming KX Alder.
Set the postcards aside for now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The card gives Wren a name to hang the mystery on, Alder, and a frequency that matches the loop to the decimal; whoever Alder is, the whole coast of listeners has been quietly hearing him for years and no one ever answered'>>

choices.label: Find out who Alder is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='Wren stacks the postcards back in the trunk and closes the lid, meaning to come back when she is less tired; morning brings school and homework and ordinary noise, and the cards, and the signal, slip quietly back into the past'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The radio shed smells of solder and dust; her grandfather tuning notes are pinned in ranks across one wall, but the filing cabinet where he kept his correspondence is padlocked, and the only key she knows of is lost somewhere in the house'>>

choices.label: Read the pinned tuning notes.
Try to force the padlocked cabinet.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='One yellowed note is circled twice, KX loop, headland relay, operator ALDER, do not give up on him; her grandfather had been trying to reach the sender for decades, and now the task, unfinished, has passed to Wren'>>

choices.label: Carry his work forward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=110 beats='The padlock will not yield and the hasp is screwed tight; without the key the cabinet keeps its secrets, and by the time Wren thinks to search for it the trail has gone cold, the loop just one more thing her grandfather understood and she never will'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The logbook, the cards, the pinned notes all converge on one stubborn fact, a man named Alder has been transmitting from the headland for a lifetime, and Wren is holding the only thread that leads back to him'>>

choices.label: Follow Alder thread to the source.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=120 beats='Papers spread across her grandfather desk, Wren traces the last line of his research to its end; there is nothing left to read and only one thing left to do, go to the headland and face the man her grandfather could never quite reach'>>

choices.label: Confront what is transmitting.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=discovery words=150 beats='However she got here, Wren arrives at the same certainty; the signal is no accident and no machine left running, it is the deliberate work of one person, K. Alder, the relay last operator, looping the same numbers and the same countdown for years because nobody has ever answered back; the who is settled, and it only sharpens the harder questions'>>

choices.label: Sit with what this means.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Wren pins Alder name to her corkboard beside the frequency and the torn word M; the loop cycles again in her headphones, patient and sad, and two ways forward open, to crack the coded numbers themselves or to learn who Alder really is'>>

choices.label: Decide how to dig deeper.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The mystery has a person at its center now, but a person is not an answer; Wren can bend her attention to the numbers Alder reads, hunting the code hidden in them, or she can chase Alder himself through the town records and the people who remember him'>>

choices.label: Crack the coded numbers.
Find out who Alder is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='Wren writes the number string out in full and stares; it could be a book cipher keyed to a text she already owns, her grandfather logbook maybe, or it could be timestamps, a countdown ticking toward one fixed moment, and each theory needs a different tool'>>

choices.label: Test it as a book cipher.
Test it as a countdown of timestamps.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Using the logbook page numbers, Wren maps each figure to a word; fragments surface, holding and answer and the letter M, enough to feel real, but one cluster stays gibberish and she must decide whether to keep forcing the logbook key or switch to a second book he underlined'>>

choices.label: Keep forcing the logbook key.
Switch to the underlined second book.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The logbook key holds; the numbers resolve into a plain, aching sentence, still holding the channel open for M, and the final group is not a word at all but a running count, the loop is measuring how long is left'>>

choices.label: See what the count is measuring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The second underlined book is a battered tide table, and against it the stubborn cluster finally speaks, a date and an hour; but the rest turns to noise on this key, and Wren must judge whether she has enough or has hit a wall'>>

choices.label: Trust the date she recovered.
Admit the cipher has beaten her.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The tide-table key gives Wren a single decoded certainty, a date and hour only days away, wrapped around the same repeated plea for M; the numbers are a clock, and the clock is nearly run down'>>

choices.label: Learn what the clock ends on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The cipher slips through Wren’s fingers, every new key spitting out fresh nonsense until the numbers blur together; she closes her notebooks well past midnight, no closer to the meaning, the loop still counting in the dark toward a total she cannot read'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Treating the string as timestamps, Wren logs the intervals between broadcasts; they are shrinking, each cycle a little shorter than the last, and she can either keep timing the loop to project its end or map the shrinking pattern against a calendar'>>

choices.label: Keep timing the shrinking intervals.
Map the pattern onto a calendar.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The intervals are collapsing on a curve; Wren plots them and the line drives toward zero within the week, the loop is not eternal after all, it is counting down, and something happens when it reaches the bottom'>>

choices.label: Find out what zero brings.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Laid over a calendar the intervals lock onto one date, and beside it in the loop sits the repeated letter M; Wren can commit to that reading and push on, or she can decide she is forcing a pattern that is not really there and stop'>>

choices.label: Commit to the date and push on.
Decide she is imagining the pattern.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Wren circles the date twice; the loop is a countdown to that exact night, threaded through with a name, and whatever Alder set in motion is meant to finish then whether anyone is listening or not'>>

choices.label: Learn what finishes that night.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Doubt wins; Wren decides she has been reading shapes into static, the way people see faces in clouds, and she puts her charts away for good; the loop keeps its rhythm without her, and she never learns she was right all along'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='To find Alder, Wren needs someone who remembers; the county library keeps microfilm of the local paper going back a century, and old Mrs. Okafor at the marina has mended nets beside that headland since before the relay was built'>>

choices.label: Search the library microfilm.
Ask Mrs. Okafor at the marina.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The microfilm reader whirs; Wren finds a decommission notice, the relay closed, its staff let go, and a photograph of a lone technician who refused a transfer, but the archive is shutting for renovation within the hour and she cannot read every reel'>>

choices.label: Read the decommission article closely.
Photograph the pages before it closes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='The article names him, Kell Alder, the one operator who stayed on unpaid after the shutdown, insisting the relay still had a job to do; the reporter thought it a sad eccentricity, but Wren reads the loop underneath it now'>>

choices.label: Understand why he stayed.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Wren shoots the pages just as the lights dim; later, zooming in, she reads the caption, K. Alder, relay operator, and a margin note in a librarian hand, he never did stop broadcasting, did he, which tells her the loop has outlived the whole town memory'>>

choices.label: Trace the loop long life.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Mrs. Okafor hands Wren a mug of tea and studies her a long moment; she knew Kell Alder, she says, knew what he lost and what he would not let go of, then she pauses, weighing whether the girl truly wants to carry this or should be sent gently home to bed'>>

choices.label: Promise her you mean to help.
Waver, and let her send you home.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=135 beats='Satisfied, Mrs. Okafor tells it plain; Alder’s daughter left after a terrible quarrel and never wrote, and he swore he would keep a channel open for her until she answered, a promise he has kept alone for thirty years'>>

choices.label: Carry his promise forward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Wren hesitates a beat too long, and Mrs. Okafor kindly decides the past is too heavy for a school night; she walks Wren to the door with a pat and a thermos, and the story of Kell Alder stays sealed in an old woman memory, the loop still calling in the cold'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=150 beats='The threads knot into one answer; the signal repeats because it is a countdown that resets every time it goes unanswered, and it is not broadcast to the world at all, it is addressed, aimed at a single listener marked only M in Alder logs; for thirty years he has held a channel open for someone who never called back, restarting the loop each time the silence won'>>

choices.label: Take in the weight of it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Wren sits back, headphones warm against her ears, the loop suddenly unbearably human; two questions remain and they braid together, who exactly is M, and what happens when this newest countdown finally reaches zero'>>

choices.label: Decide which question to chase.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='She cannot answer a signal she does not understand; Wren can hunt down who M really is, the listener Alder has waited a lifetime for, or she can work out exactly what the shrinking countdown is racing toward'>>

choices.label: Find out who M is.
Find out what the countdown reaches.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='M could be a person or a place; Wren can dig into Alder family, the daughter Mrs. Okafor mentioned, or she can test the darker thought that M is a coordinate, a landfall he keeps naming for a ship or a soul that never arrives'>>

choices.label: Search Alder family for M.
Test M as a place on the map.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='Public records give Wren a daughter, Marin Alder, gone from the county three decades back; there is also a sister listed as next of kin, still local, and Wren must choose which Alder to pin her hope on with the clock already shrinking'>>

choices.label: Trace the daughter, Marin.
Trace the local sister instead.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Marin is the M, Alder’s daughter, who walked out after their last fight and has drifted from town to town since; the loop is his standing apology, broadcast on the frequency they once shared, waiting for the day she tunes back in'>>

choices.label: Fix on reaching Marin.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The sister, reached by phone, weeps softly; M is Marin, she confirms, Kell only child, and she gives Wren the last address she has, a long shot two towns west, and one plea, please, before it is too late for them both'>>

choices.label: Race the sister long shot.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='On the chart, M could mark a mooring off the headland where a boat once went down; Wren can chase that landfall reading, or she can admit the map is leading her in circles while the real answer is a person, not a place'>>

choices.label: Check the mooring the chart shows.
Give up the map as a dead end.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The mooring is a memorial buoy, and its plaque carries a name, Marin, painted the year Alder’s daughter vanished; M is not a place after all but a person the whole headland has quietly mourned, and she may yet be alive to hear him'>>

choices.label: Turn the search toward Marin herself.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Wren chases the map in widening circles until every lead folds back on itself; exhausted, she saves a clean recording of the loop to her phone and lets the search go, carrying a mystery she can play for people but never finish'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=135 beats='To pin the countdown target, Wren can extrapolate the shrinking loop straight to its zero point, or she can test her hunch that it is timed to the coast itself, to a tide or a storm swelling toward the headland this very week'>>

choices.label: Extrapolate the loop to zero.
Test it against the tide and weather.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The math lands the zero point on a night only days off; but a countdown needs a thing to reach, and Wren must decide whether zero marks the relay failing power cell finally dying or a message Alder set to play automatically when the wait runs out'>>

choices.label: Read zero as the power cell dying.
Read zero as a final message firing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='The relay runs on a decades-old power cell, and Wren extrapolation matches its rated end almost to the hour; when it dies the loop dies with it, and Alder thirty-year channel to Marin goes dark forever unless someone acts first'>>

choices.label: Weigh what dark forever means.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Buried in the logs Wren finds it, a scheduled final transmission, one last message queued to play when the countdown ends and then erase itself, Alder goodbye to Marin, set to speak once into the dark and be gone'>>

choices.label: Resolve to catch it before it erases.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=130 beats='The countdown does align with an incoming spring tide and a gale forecast for the headland; that could mean the storm will simply drown the failing relay, and Wren can raise the alarm about it or hold back, unsure she has the story right'>>

choices.label: Warn someone about the storm and relay.
Hold back until she is certain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=125 beats='Wren connects it, the gale will swamp the headland the same night the cell dies, ending the loop twice over; the countdown is real and physical now, weather and machinery closing on one hour, and the window to act is almost shut'>>

choices.label: Move before the window closes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Wren decides she needs to be sure before she troubles anyone, so she waits one more night to double-check her figures; in the morning the loop is gone, only clean hiss where the countdown used to be, the relay silenced while she hesitated'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=155 beats='Everything Wren has gathered snaps into one clear, ticking shape; the countdown ends this coming night, when the relay ancient power cell finally fails, and the moment it does a last message will play, Kell Alder recorded farewell to his daughter Marin, and then erase itself into silence with the storm rolling in behind it; thirty years of waiting come down to one hour, and Wren is the only person who knows'>>

choices.label: Decide how to spend that hour.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=130 beats='Rain begins to freckle the window as Wren weighs her options; the headland is a bike ride away, her grandfather transmitter still works, and the town has people who could move faster than she can, but every path runs against the same shrinking clock'>>

choices.label: Choose an approach before zero.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='One hour, three ways to spend it; Wren can race to the relay station itself and answer the signal at its source, she can use her grandfather rig to rebroadcast the message wide enough that Marin might hear it, or she can rouse the people who could reach the headland faster and surer than a girl on a bike in a gale'>>

choices.label: Race to the relay station.
Rebroadcast the message yourself.
Get help from people who can reach it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren pedals into the rising storm and reaches the relay gate chained shut; the mast light pulses with the loop above her, minutes left on the clock, and she can go over the rattling fence into the yard or gamble on the service road that curls to a back entrance'>>

choices.label: Go over the fence into the yard.
Take the service road to the back.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren tops the fence and drops into the flooded yard; the control room door hangs ajar ahead, spilling faint light, but the exposed climb has cost her precious minutes and the wind is trying to peel her off the concrete, so she can push straight for that door or catch her breath first'>>

choices.label: Push straight for the control room.
Freeze against the wind, hesitating.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=145 beats='Inside, the control room is a shrine of dials and one glowing console counting down in red; Wren can seize the microphone and answer the transmitter directly, or she can see the failing power cell guttering and try to stabilize it before it dies mid-message'>>

choices.label: Seize the mic and answer the signal.
Try to stabilize the dying power cell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=145 beats='The countdown reads under a minute; Wren finds Alder patched contact list open on the desk, Marin newest number circled and never dialed, and she can bridge that line and let the recorded message finally reach a living ear, or simply key the transmitter to halt the wipe and save the whole archive of his waiting'>>

choices.label: Bridge the line to Marin and play it.
Halt the wipe and save the archive.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='Wren dials the circled number and patches it through as the counter hits zero; across three towns a phone rings, a startled woman answers, and her father recorded voice, thirty years patient, fills the line, still holding the channel open for you, Marin, and this time somebody is there to hear it'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='Wren keys the override a breath before the wipe; the countdown freezes, the loop steadies, and thirty years of Alder logs and messages are saved intact, a whole patient archive of love preserved so that when Marin is found, every word he sent will still be waiting for her'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='The power cell is a corroded brick barely holding voltage; Wren remembers her grandfather spare battery in her pack and can splice it in to buy the relay hours and then call the whole thing in properly, or she can nurse the dying cell by hand and pray it lasts through one last broadcast'>>

choices.label: Splice in the spare and call it in.
Nurse the dying cell by hand.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='The spliced cell catches and the console steadies; Wren gets the relay stable, then phones the coast radio club with the whole story, and by dawn Alder loop is safe on backup power while volunteers begin the search for Marin, the countdown stopped and the waiting handed on to people who can finish it'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren’s hands are steady but the cell is too far gone; it fails with a small final click just as the message begins, cutting Alder’s farewell to a single word, Marin, before the room goes dark and silent, though Wren’s clean recording of that last word is all that survives the night'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Pinned by the wind at the yard edge, Wren loses her nerve and her seconds together; by the time she forces herself to the door the console has already gone dark, the countdown spent, and she is left in a dead control room with a mystery solved one minute too late'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='The service road is longer but sheltered, and Wren pedals hard around to a back door standing open; a lamp burns inside, and there, hunched over the console he has tended for thirty years, is an old man she can only be Kell Alder himself, or she finds the door locked after all and time bleeding away'>>

choices.label: Find Alder himself at the console.
Find the back door locked, and wait.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=145 beats='Kell Alder turns, startled to find a girl dripping in his doorway; Wren blurts everything she has pieced together, the loop, the countdown, Marin, and she can beg him to let the message go out live tonight while there is still power, or watch him shake his head, certain after thirty years that no one is really listening'>>

choices.label: Convince Alder to send it live.
Fail to move him, and leave a note.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='Something in Wren certainty reaches the old man; Alder wipes his eyes, leans to the mic, and instead of the recording speaks live for the first time in decades, telling the open channel he is still here, still sorry, still waiting, and two towns west a woman who never stopped listening finally lifts the handset'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Alder only shakes his head; thirty years of silence have convinced him no answer is coming, and he will not risk hoping again tonight, not even for a stranger. Wren leaves her notebook of everything she found on his desk and bikes home in the rain, the choice, and the loop, left in his tired hands'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The back door is bolted and no knock brings anyone; Wren huddles under the eave counting the minutes until the mast light stutters and goes still, the countdown finished on the far side of a locked door, so close she could hear the last tone fade through the wall'>>

ending.title: On the Wrong Side of the Door

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren stays home and runs for the attic instead; if she cannot reach the headland in time, she can amplify Alder message so far and wide that Marin might stumble on it, using her grandfather own transmitter, or she can wake the town amateur radio club to throw it across their whole county net'>>

choices.label: Use your grandfather transmitter.
Call the town radio club net.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Her grandfather rig hums to life under Wren hands, warm and familiar; she can boost the relay loop and splice Marin name across every band she can legally reach, praying it carries, or she can push the old set past its limits for more range than it was ever built to give'>>

choices.label: Boost the loop and call for Marin.
Push the old rig past its limits.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren rebroadcasts Alder message on a steady boost, Marin name riding the loop out across the dark coast; for a long minute there is only hiss, and then she must decide whether the faint carrier swelling on the return frequency is truly an answer or just her own hope, and whether to keep calling'>>

choices.label: Answer the carrier swelling back.
Trust a neighbor heard, and stop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='The carrier resolves into a voice, hesitant and disbelieving, asking who is broadcasting her name; Wren explains in a rush, her father, the relay, thirty years, and hands the channel to the loop, and Marin listens to her father waiting message with a stranger girl as witness, the silence finally, gently broken'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='No answer comes for Marin, but a neighbor two streets over hears the whole broadcast and calls, shaken, promising to spread it through every relay and club she knows; Wren has not closed the loop herself, yet she has thrown Alder message far past the headland, and now a hundred listeners will carry it on'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Wren pushes the old rig too hard and it dies in a wisp of blue smoke and the smell of scorched solder; the attic goes quiet, her grandfather transmitter finished the same night as Alder loop, and she is left with a dark set and a message she could not send'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren rings the amateur radio club emergency line; a sleepy operator answers, then wakes fully as the story spills out, and he can throw Alder message onto the county-wide net at once for maximum reach, or she can hear that no one is on shift and only a recorder is listening at this hour'>>

choices.label: Push it onto the county net now.
Reach only the after-hours recorder.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='The club fires Alder message across the whole county net, dozens of stations relaying Marin name into the storm; within minutes reports trickle back, and Wren must judge whether the strong relay from a town two ridges west, where a woman is asking urgent questions, is the lead she has prayed for or a coincidence to chase'>>

choices.label: Chase the lead two ridges west.
Log every lead for the club to follow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='The lead is real; the woman two ridges west is Marin, up late and idly scanning the bands she abandoned decades ago, and the sound of her own name in her father old loop stops her cold. The club patches her through to the relay, and Kell Alder thirty-year wait ends in a single trembling hello'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='Wren cannot be sure any single lead is Marin, so she logs them all and hands the club a complete file, the frequency, the story, every promising callback; the net keeps the message circulating night after night, and though Wren does not see the ending herself, Alder loop is finally in a hundred searching hands'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Only the after-hours recorder answers, and Wren leaves the whole story into the void, hoping someone plays it back at dawn; but dawn is after the countdown, and by the time a club volunteer hears her message the relay loop has already fallen silent, her call arriving a night too late'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren decides she cannot do this alone against the clock; she can phone the coast guard auxiliary, who treat any headland beacon seriously and can reach the relay by road in minutes, or she can track down Alder old colleague from the relay days, a man who would understand every word of what she has found'>>

choices.label: Call the coast guard auxiliary.
Find Alder old colleague.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='The auxiliary duty officer takes Wren seriously the moment she says a beacon on the headland is counting down in a storm; he can dispatch a crew to the relay to secure it as a live signal, or he can hesitate, unable to log a mystery loop as a real emergency without more than a schoolgirl word'>>

choices.label: Convince him to dispatch a crew.
Watch him dismiss it as static.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='A crew rolls for the headland with Wren guiding them by phone; reaching the relay ahead of the storm, they radio back that the console is alive and counting, and Wren must tell them what matters most in the last minute, to trace the message intended listener or to preserve the failing station whole'>>

choices.label: Have them trace and reach Marin.
Have them secure and save the relay.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='The crew patches Wren straight into the relay, and with their gear behind Alder thirty-year loop the signal leaps its old limits; they trace the return call to Marin within the hour and hold the channel open until father and daughter are, at last and across all that silence, speaking to each other'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=125 beats='The crew secures the relay minutes before the storm hits, swapping the dead cell and locking the loop safely onto backup power; nothing is lost, and with the station stable the auxiliary opens a proper search for Marin, crediting the whole discovery to the girl who would not let a strange signal go'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='Without a real emergency to log, the officer thanks Wren politely and files her call under curiosities; no crew rolls, and out on the headland the countdown runs down unwatched, the loop dying in the gale while the one report that could have saved it sits closed in a duty logbook'>>

<<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Wren tracks down Alder old relay colleague, Ruiz, retired across the bay; he goes quiet when she describes the loop, then says he always feared Kell would do this, and he can drive out to help her reach the station in time, or she can learn his number is years dead and the man long moved away'>>

choices.label: Team up with Ruiz to reach the relay.
Find the colleague long gone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=140 beats='Ruiz meets Wren at the headland gate with keys he never returned; he knows the console cold, and together they can complete Alder scheduled transmission the right way before the cell dies, or Wren can step back and let the old professional take it from here, her part in the search already done'>>

choices.label: Complete the transmission together.
Step back and let Ruiz finish it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=130 beats='Side by side, Wren and Ruiz bring the relay to full power and send Alder message out clean and strong on the exact frequency Marin once knew; when the answer comes, faint then sure, Ruiz grips Wren shoulder, and the loop that counted down alone for thirty years ends with two people there to hear it land'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=120 beats='Wren hands Ruiz the story and steps back, and he takes over with the ease of a man who built the place; she bikes home certain it is in good hands, yet she never hears how it ends, whether Marin answered or the loop simply stopped, her long night closing on a question she will always carry'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=115 beats='The number rings out to a stranger; Ruiz moved away years ago and left no forwarding line, and every other name from the relay days has scattered or passed on. Wren sits with the dead phone as the clock runs down, the one person who could have helped her already gone beyond reach'>>
